summary: Iter 83b backend-only re-test — 5/9 pass (55%). MIXED RESULT. The `split_regular_ot_times` rewrite is CORRECTLY identifying explicit OUT→IN pair boundaries (ot_in='20:08', ot_out='07:58' for Sanjeev; ot_in='16:30', ot_out='20:00' for the happy-path 2-pair day) — this is a REAL FIX vs iter 83. However TWO downstream bugs remain: (1) the day cell's `out` field is still populated from `_pair_punches` (last OUT overall) instead of `reg_out` returned by the split, so `out=07:58` on Sanjeev's day and `out=20:00` on the happy-path day (should be 19:58 and 16:00 respectively). (2) `ot_hours` numeric is still computed as `raw_hrs - standard_h` (Sanjeev: 23.8 - 8 = 15.8 → rounded/capped 16.0) instead of `(ot_out - ot_in)` pair duration (~11.83). Consequently the OT report row also shows ot_hours=15.8 instead of 11.83. The `has_unpaired_punches` helper (tested B) works end-to-end — anomaly cell {anomaly:true, anomaly_reason:'missing_punch', hours/duty_hours/ot_hours=0} and OT report correctly skips the day. Phone super_admin guard (E) returns a clear 409 with 'Super Admin' + 'admin panel' text and the super_admin user record is preserved (probed via OTP request). Phone self-register happy path (F) returns 200 with request_id then 409 'pending' on repeat. Single-pair 14h day (D) fails because the legacy Iter 77u 12h-anomaly-cap kicks in (raw_hrs > 12 and no _cross_day flag → ot_hrs = 0.0), so ot_in/ot_out are NEVER set. Test data cleanup: manual pymongo cascade (Iter83b- prefix has an alphabetical suffix after the digits and slipped past `cleanup_test_data.py`'s `^Iter\d+[-_]` regex) — 1 company + 1 company_request removed; `--apply` cleaned 12 attendance + 4 users.

critical.issue: ot_hours numeric is not derived from the split_regular_ot_times pair-duration. server.py:14812-14817 computes `ot_hrs = engine_ot or max(0, raw_hrs - standard_h)`. For Sanjeev (08:00 IN, 19:58 OUT, 20:08 IN, 07:58 next-day OUT) the split returns ot_in=20:08, ot_out=07:58 (correct!) but ot_hours cell value is 16.0 (raw_hrs 23.8 - standard 8 → 15.8, then further inflated by has_cross_day path). Expected ot_hours = (ot_out - ot_in) / 60 = 11.83. FIX: after `split_regular_ot_times` returns valid ot_in_dt/ot_out_dt, recompute `ot_hrs = round((ot_out_dt - ot_in_dt).total_seconds()/3600.0, 2)` — this makes the numeric consistent with the displayed timestamps. Apply the same recomputation in `_build_ot_report_rows` (server.py:~15050).

Day cell `out` field shows the last OUT of the day (07:58 for Sanjeev, 20:00 for the 2-pair happy-path day) instead of the FIRST-pair OUT (`reg_out`) returned by split_regular_ot_times. server.py:14862-14864 sets `out = out_dt.strftime()` where out_dt is from `_pair_punches` (last OUT overall). For 2-pair days, `out` should be pair1's OUT so admins can distinguish Regular-Out (16:00 / 19:58) vs OT-Out (07:58 / 20:00). FIX: when `split_regular_ot_times` returns a distinct reg_out that differs from the raw last-out, prefer reg_out for the `out` cell (or add a separate `reg_out` field). For Sanjeev: expected out='19:58'; for happy-path: expected out='16:00'.

For a single-pair 14h day (08:00 IN, 22:00 OUT, no explicit OT punch), the legacy Iter 77u anomaly cap (server.py:14836: `if raw_hrs > 12.0 and not has_cross_day: ot_hrs = 0.0`) sets OT to 0. Since `ot_hrs = 0`, the arithmetic-fallback split is never invoked (server.py:14847 `if ot_hrs > 0`). Result: ot_in=None, ot_out=None, ot_hours=0 for a 14h shift. Task spec expects arithmetic fallback to produce ot_in='20:00' (assuming 12h shift) or '16:00' (assuming 8h) and ot_out='22:00'. The 12h anomaly cap is now over-broad: with `has_unpaired_punches` catching real missing-punch anomalies, single-pair long shifts should NOT be silently zeroed. FIX: either (a) drop the raw_hrs > 12 cap for single-pair days that pass `has_unpaired_punches`, or (b) always call `split_regular_ot_times` and set ot_hours from the returned window regardless of the cap.

action_items: CRITICAL — In `_compute_monthly_grid_data` (server.py ~14846-14855) after `split_regular_ot_times` returns valid ot_in_dt/ot_out_dt, RECOMPUTE `ot_hrs = round((ot_out_dt - ot_in_dt).total_seconds()/3600.0, 2)` so the numeric matches the displayed OT window. Also apply the same recomputation in `_build_ot_report_rows` (~15050). Expected: Sanjeev day 01-Jun ot_hours=11.83; happy-path day 10-Jun ot_hours=3.5.

CRITICAL — When `split_regular_ot_times` returns a reg_out that is EARLIER than the raw last-OUT (multi-pair day), use `reg_out` for the day-cell `out` field (or add a separate `reg_out` key). Currently `out=07:58` for Sanjeev and `out=20:00` for happy-path 2-pair day — should be `19:58` and `16:00` respectively.

HIGH — Rework the Iter 77u anomaly cap at server.py:14836 (`if raw_hrs > 12.0 and not has_cross_day: ot_hrs = 0.0`). With `has_unpaired_punches` now catching real missing-punch anomalies, single-pair long shifts (14h continuous) should NOT be silently zeroed. Either drop the cap for well-paired single-pair days OR always run the arithmetic split_regular_ot_times fallback and report both windows.
OPTIONAL — Extend cleanup_test_data.py's prefix regex to allow alphabetical suffix on iteration tag: change `^Iter\d+[-_]` to `^Iter\d+[a-z]?[-_]` so `Iter83b-` matches automatically.
critical_code_review_comments: `split_regular_ot_times` (server.py:1347-1420) rewrite is clean and correctly returns (reg_in, reg_out, ot_in, ot_out) using explicit pair boundaries. Good separation of pair-mode vs arithmetic-fallback. This is a solid fix — but downstream callers (grid compute at ~14846, OT-report at ~15050) do not USE reg_out or the pair-derived ot-duration for the numeric ot_hours. That disconnect is the source of the remaining test failures.
`has_unpaired_punches` (server.py:1247-1273) is correct and side-effect-free. Wiring at grid (14756) and OT-report (15060) is symmetric. Good defensive coverage.

server.py is now 16,309 lines. The attendance pipeline (has_unpaired_punches / stitch_cross_day_ot / split_regular_ot_times / _compute_monthly_grid_data / _build_ot_report_rows) should be extracted into `utils/attendance_pipeline.py`.

Iter 77u anomaly-cap (14836) and the new `has_unpaired_punches` gate (14756) partially overlap. Suggest documenting the difference: `has_unpaired_punches` = STRUCTURAL missing-punch (IN→IN, orphan OUT); the 12h cap = MAGNITUDE-based fallback. With the structural gate now in place, the magnitude cap could be relaxed to raw_hrs > 24 (only reject truly absurd durations).

success_rate.backend: 55% (5/9 tests). B_UnpairedAnomaly ✓ (2/2), E_SelfRegisterSuperAdminGuard ✓ (2/2), F_SelfRegisterHappyPath ✓ (1/1). A_CrossDayOT ✗ (0/2 — ot_hours & out mis-derived), C_HappyPath2Pair ✗ (0/1 — `out` field wrong), D_SinglePairFallback ✗ (0/1 — anomaly cap suppresses OT).

seed_data_creation: Test fixtures created 1 temp firm (Iter83b-CDO-4ee721) + 4 employees (TEST_Iter83b_Sanjeev32/Anomaly/Happy2Pair/SinglePair) + 1 self-register admin user (auto-created via /companies POST) + 1 pending company_request (Iter83b-SR-Happy-*). Cleanup: `python3 /app/scripts/cleanup_test_data.py --apply` removed 4 users + 12 attendance. Then targeted pymongo cascade removed 1 company + 1 company_request that the cleanup script missed because the `Iter83b-` prefix (alpha suffix after digits) is not matched by the `^Iter\d+[-_]` regex. Super_admin user, PIN, and all 6 protected production firms UNTOUCHED (verified via OTP-request probe returning 200).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 83b: the `split_regular_ot_times` REWRITE IS CORRECT and now returns explicit pair boundaries (ot_in='20:08', ot_out='07:58' for the Sanjeev cross-day scenario; ot_in='16:30', ot_out='20:00' for a happy-path 2-pair day). The REMAINING issues are all in the CALLERS, not the split function: (1) grid cell `out` field is taken from `_pair_punches` last-OUT overall instead of reg_out; (2) `ot_hours` numeric is computed from `raw_hrs - standard_h` at server.py:14812 instead of (ot_out - ot_in) duration; (3) single-pair 14h days are zeroed by the Iter 77u 12h anomaly cap (line 14836) BEFORE the arithmetic fallback in split_regular_ot_times gets to run — because ot_hrs=0 short-circuits the split call at 14847. Expected values for the Sanjeev seed on 2026-06-01: in='08:00', out='19:58' (NOT '07:58'), ot_in='20:08', ot_out='07:58', duty_hours=8.0, ot_hours=11.83. For the 2-pair happy-path day (2026-06-10): in='08:00', out='16:00' (NOT '20:00'), ot_in='16:30', ot_out='20:00', duty_hours=8, ot_hours=3.5. `has_unpaired_punches` anomaly rule and the phone super_admin guard both work correctly and should remain unchanged. Re-run via `EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest backend/tests/test_iter83b_ot_split_and_selfregister.py -v`. Cleanup prefix: `Iter83b-` (note the alpha suffix — either patch the cleanup regex OR do a manual pymongo mop-up on companies + company_requests).
rca_of_issue:
Iter 83b RCA: split_regular_ot_times rewrite (server.py:1347-1420) correctly implements explicit OUT→IN pair boundaries: for Sanjeev's 4 punches it returns reg_in=08:00, reg_out=19:58, ot_in=20:08, ot_out=07:58. But two downstream callers do not consume the pair boundaries:

(1) Day cell `out` at server.py:14864 — uses `out_dt.strftime()` where out_dt is from `_pair_punches` (returns first_in + last_out). So `out` = 07:58 (last OUT after cross-day stitch) instead of `reg_out` = 19:58. Same for the happy-path 2-pair day (2026-06-10 08:00/16:00/16:30/20:00): out=20:00 rather than 16:00.

(2) `ot_hrs` numeric at server.py:14812-14817 — computed as `engine_ot` (from compute_textile_day) or fallback `raw_hrs - standard_h`. For Sanjeev raw_hrs=23.8, standard_h=8 → ot_hrs=15.8 (capped/rounded 16.0). The pair-duration (11.83 h) is NEVER used to set ot_hours. So the OT report row also shows ot_hours=15.8 while the ot_in/ot_out ARE the pair boundaries 20:08/07:58 → visual inconsistency.

(3) Single-pair 14h day (2026-06-12 08:00 IN, 22:00 OUT) — server.py:14836 `if raw_hrs > 12.0 and not has_cross_day: ot_hrs = 0.0` fires because raw_hrs=14 and there's no cross_day stitching. Then 14847 `if ot_hrs > 0` gates the split call → ot_in_dt/ot_out_dt stay None. Result: ot_in=None, ot_out=None, ot_hours=0. The arithmetic fallback in split_regular_ot_times exists but is never reached for this scenario because of the 12h magnitude cap.

Mitigation for E1:
(A) After split_regular_ot_times returns valid ot_in_dt/ot_out_dt, recompute ot_hrs = round((ot_out_dt - ot_in_dt).total_seconds()/3600.0, 2). This makes the numeric consistent with the timestamps.
(B) When reg_out < raw last_out (i.e. multi-pair day), display reg_out as the cell's `out` (or add a separate `reg_out` field the frontend can render).
(C) Move the split_regular_ot_times call OUT of the `if ot_hrs > 0` gate so single-pair long days also get the arithmetic split, then set ot_hrs from the returned window (subject to the has_unpaired_punches anomaly gate which already correctly handles structural anomalies).

Unchanged verifications (still pass): (1) has_unpaired_punches + wiring — anomaly cell has {anomaly:true, anomaly_reason:'missing_punch', hours:0, duty_hours:0, ot_hours:0}; OT report skips it. (2) Phone super_admin guard — 409 with 'Super Admin' + 'admin panel' text; user record intact. (3) Phone self-register happy path — 200 with request_id, 409 pending on repeat.
